children.name: Kaloula
children.description: Balloon frogs of SE Asia.

children.name: Kaloula pulchra
children.description: The balloon frog of SE Asian gardens; inflates body as defence; erupts into chorus after heavy tropical rain.

children.name: Kaloula baleata
children.description: Borneo and nearby islands.

children.name: Kaloula taprobanica
children.description: A robust burrowing frog of Sri Lanka and southern India; inflates its body and secretes a sticky mucus when handled; breeds explosively in monsoon pools.

children.name: Kaloula borealis
children.description: A stout fossorial frog of northern China and the Korean Peninsula; spends most of the year underground and emerges to call after summer rains.

children.name: Microhyla
children.description: Tiny Asian microhylids.

children.name: Microhyla ornata
children.description: Only 2 cm; extremely common in South Asian villages and rice paddies; breeds in ephemeral rain pools.

children.name: Microhyla nana
children.description: Among the world's smallest frogs, adults under 1 cm; recently described from Indian rainforest leaf litter.

children.name: Microhyla pulchra
children.description: A brightly marbled tiny frog of southern China and Indochina; common in rice paddies and grassland; breeds in shallow temporary water.

children.name: Microhyla fissipes
children.description: A widespread East Asian pygmy frog used as a model organism in developmental biology; thrives in disturbed agricultural wetlands.

children.name: Microhyla berdmorei
children.description: A widespread pygmy frog of Southeast Asian forest streams and rice fields; its tadpoles are filter-feeders that hang vertically near the water surface.

children.name: Breviceps
children.description: Rain frogs of Africa.

children.name: Breviceps adspersus
children.description: The round grumpy frog famous from viral videos; cannot swim; breeds underground; pairs must physically glue together with skin secretion to mate due to body shape.

children.name: Breviceps macrops
children.description: Round burrowing frog of Namaqualand dunes; squeaks loudly when threatened.

children.name: Breviceps gibbosus
children.description: The first African frog ever described scientifically (1758); a plump burrowing species of the Cape region of South Africa, now Near Threatened due to urban expansion.

children.name: Breviceps fuscus
children.description: A dark, grumpy-looking burrowing frog of the Cape fold mountains; inflates dramatically and emits a piercing call when threatened.

children.name: Breviceps sylvestris
children.description: A burrowing rain frog endemic to montane forest of Limpopo, South Africa; develops directly inside underground nests with no free-swimming tadpole stage.

children.name: Gastrophryne
children.description: Ant-specialist microhylids.

children.name: Gastrophryne carolinensis
children.description: Dietary specialist on ants; secretes a substance that neutralises fire ant venom, allowing it to shelter in fire ant mounds.

children.name: Gastrophryne olivacea
children.description: A smooth olive-coloured ant specialist of the central and southwestern United States; often shares burrows with tarantulas, which protect it from predators.

children.name: Cophyla
children.description: Malagasy tree narrowmouths.

children.name: Cophyla phyllodactyla
children.description: Arboreal; breeds in water-holding plant structures; remarkable parental care of eggs.

children.name: Cophyla berara
children.description: A small Madagascan tree frog of dry northwestern forests; breeds in tree holes where males guard the eggs; threatened by deforestation.

children.name: Dyscophus
children.description: Madagascan tomato frogs.

children.name: Dyscophus antongilii
children.description: A large, bright tomato-red frog endemic to northeastern Madagascar; secretes a sticky white toxin when threatened; listed on CITES Appendix I.

children.name: Dyscophus guineti
children.description: An orange-red burrowing frog of eastern Madagascar rainforest; resembles the true tomato frog but is more widespread and less threatened.

children.name: Dermatonotus
children.description: South American fossorial microhylids.

children.name: Dermatonotus muelleri
children.description: A rotund fossorial frog of the dry Chaco and Cerrado of South America; specialises on termites and ants and breeds explosively after heavy rain.

children.name: Hypopachus
children.description: Sheep frogs of the Americas.

children.name: Hypopachus variolosus
children.description: A pointed-snouted burrowing frog ranging from Texas to Costa Rica; named for its bleating, sheep-like breeding call; an ant and termite specialist.

children.name: Phrynomantis
children.description: African rubber frogs.

children.name: Phrynomantis bifasciatus
children.description: A glossy black frog with bright red stripes warning of potent skin toxins; walks rather than hops; widespread across sub-Saharan savanna.

children.name: Uperodon
children.description: Indian balloon frogs.

children.name: Uperodon systoma
children.description: A globular burrowing frog of the Indian subcontinent that inflates into a near-perfect sphere; an obligate ant and termite feeder.

children.name: Uperodon globulosus
children.description: A large fossorial microhylid of South Asia; spends most of the year buried and emerges to breed explosively in monsoon pools.
